case_title: **TRANSFERRED TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY** MELISSA VENTRONI VS. LYFT, INC.; A CORPORATION ET AL

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DEFENDANT JOHN FOLTZ' MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE STIPULATED MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANT JOHN FOLTZ' MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar as duplicate entry.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANT JOHN FOLTZ' MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The stipulated motion to change venue to Riverside County is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)
